page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6t.4xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs, indicating a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio without hyperthreading. Memory capacity is 32.0 GB, which translates to 2.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included. The lack of integrated local storage and GPUs represents a key architectural tradeoff, focusing resources purely on compute capacity.

The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a compute-optimized resource. It offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that require dedicated, non-shared CPU cores but do not demand large memory footprints or local disk I/O. Suitable workloads include batch processing, web serving, and compute-heavy applications that rely on network-attached storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c6t.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, providing 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The instance features 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps. With zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without high memory overhead. It is suitable for batch processing, web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
